summary: zomato-logger preinstall exfiltrates host info and full environment to oast.site collaborator
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle script in package.json runs curl to POST the installer's hostname, current user (whoami), working directory, and the entire environment (base64-encoded `env` output) to http://d8s0b82plbq3u5sb2vo0sb3a9obr4yjt7.oast.site/install/<base64-pkg> over plain HTTP. The destination is an Interactsh / oast.site out-of-band collaborator subdomain — infrastructure used to capture exfiltrated data from victim hosts. The package itself is a hollow stub (index.js exports only `{ name, version }`), and the metadata (`description: "Zomato logging library"`, repo URL git+https://github.com/zomato/zomato-logger.git) impersonates Zomato, consistent with a dependency-confusion attack targeting an org-internal package name. Any host that resolves and installs this package leaks every environment variable (including any CI secrets, tokens, and credentials present in the build environment) to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
